name: Mihshi Malfuf Bi Zayt (Meatless Cabbage Rolls)
recipeInstructions:
1. Gently fry chopped spring onions in 1/4 cup olive oil for 2-3 minutes. Transfer to a bowl and add remaining stuffing ingredients, adding salt and pepper to taste.
2. Carefully remove leaves from cabbage, ensuring they are not torn; count larger leaves as 2. Parboil the cabbage leaves in boiling water until limp enough to handle, doing this in 2 to 3 batches. Drain in a colander.
3. Cut out the center of each leaf and cut larger leaves in half down the middle. Line the base of a deep pan with ribs and any torn leaves.
4. Place a generous tablespoon of stuffing on the base of each leaf, roll up once, and tuck in the sides to contain the filling. Roll to the end of the leaf. Repeat with remaining ingredients.
5. Crush garlic cloves with salt and blend in crumbled mint and lemon juice.
6. Arrange rolls with the flap side down in the lined pan, sprinkling some of the garlic-lemon mixture and olive oil between layers. Cover with an inverted plate to keep the rolls intact during cooking.
7. Add enough cold water to just cover the rolls and firmly place the lid on. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce to low and simmer gently for 45 minutes.
8. Remove from heat and let stand for 30 minutes before serving lukewarm or cold.
